Label18.name: Label18
Label18.label: //The following controls are only available During Conduct Audit Activities and Results (Label Hidden)

Label35.name: Label35
Label35.label: Findings that are selected together in the corresponding dropdown below will create one quality event or CAPA instance.  To create separate instances, select the findings one by one and click on either the Create Quality Event or Create CAPA button after each selection. A finding can only be selected and triggered once.

Label31.name: Label31
Label31.label: //The below controls are for scheduling activities.  (Label Hidden)

CheckBox1.name: CheckBox1
CheckBox1.label: Select to mark all requirements as verified
CheckBox1.tooltip: If the requirement checklist remains incomplete, validation from this field will be omitted.

Grid.name: Grid
Grid.label: You've been tasked with verifying the assigned requirements. To do so, double click on each row to open the 'Validate Requirement: Capture Evidence and/or Create Finding's form.

Label1.name: Label1
Label1.label: The meeting request was not created. You can send the request again by clicking the button on the right.

Label6.name: Label6
Label6.label: Selected Lead Collector will be auto-assigned to all requirements, but the user can still change the collector directly in the grid.

Label22.name: Label22
Label22.label: Assign the evidence collector(s) for each requirement
Label22.tooltip:
1. Click on "Select All Evidence" to select all the Evidence. 
2. Click on Deselect All Evidence to uncheck all rows in the grid.
3. To assign a different collector per requirement, you have to select the collector directly from the grid and click on Save Changes.

DefinitionsGrid.name: DefinitionsGrid
DefinitionsGrid.label: Assign the PREDEFINED AUDIT PROGRAM(s) as a part of this Audit
DefinitionsGrid.tooltip: Your access is limited to the audit definitions for which you hold the position of team leader.

Label2.name: Label2
Label2.label: The evidence collection process will only be triggered for requirements that require testing.

Label21.name: Label21
Label21.label: Assign auditor(s) for each requirement.
Label21.tooltip:
1. Click on Select All Requirements to assign the same auditor to all requirements
2. Click on Deselect All Requirements to uncheck all rows in the grid
3. To assign a different auditor per requirement, select in the grid only the requirements that will be assigned to the same auditor.

Label5.name: Label5
Label5.label: Once the user selects the Lead Auditor, all the requirements will be automatically assigned to the Lead Auditor. However, the user or (Lead Auditor) can still edit the Auditor directly from the Requirement grid.

Label4.name: Label4
Label4.label: Assign the auditor(s) for each requirement.
Label4.tooltip: To Assign a different Auditor per requirement, Edit the auditor directly from the grid and click on save changes.

selectRegion.name: selectRegion
selectRegion.label: Region
selectRegion.tooltip: Select the region relevant to the audit. Your choice will determine the available options in the Country dropdown.

SelectCountry.name: SelectCountry
SelectCountry.label: Country
SelectCountry.tooltip: Select the country relevant to the audit. Your options are filtered based on your Region selection.

CheckIsLocationOverride.name: CheckIsLocationOverride
CheckIsLocationOverride.label: Set this location for the audit(s) selected for execution

Label3.name: Label3
Label3.label: Specify the dates for each round of evidence collection.

DateTimePicker.name: DateTimePicker
DateTimePicker.label: Planned Start Date
DateTimePicker.tooltip: Specify the date when the audit activities will commence. This date marks the beginning of the audit execution phase.

DateTimePicker4.name: DateTimePicker4
DateTimePicker4.label: Planned End Date
DateTimePicker4.tooltip: Specify the date when the audit activities will conclude. This date marks the end of the audit execution phase.

ScheduleStart.name: ScheduleStart
ScheduleStart.label: Actual Start Date
ScheduleStart.tooltip: Specify the starting date for scheduling the audit. This date marks when the audit scheduling phase should begin to ensure a timely and organized audit execution.

UnscheduleStart.name: UnscheduleStart
UnscheduleStart.label: Actual Start Date
UnscheduleStart.tooltip: Specify the starting date for scheduling the audit. This date marks when the audit scheduling phase should begin to ensure a timely and organized audit execution.

Label16.name: Label16
Label16.label: By confirming the schedule, you acknowledge that all auditees have confirmed the audit time.

SingleSelectDropdown1.name: SingleSelectDropdown1
SingleSelectDropdown1.label: QE / CAPA Reviewer
SingleSelectDropdown1.tooltip: The selected user will receive the task to review either the quality event or CAPA created.

Label25.name: Label25
Label25.label: Code on this label hides Test required checkboxes on certain form behviours

AllAuditorsDD.name: AllAuditorsDD
AllAuditorsDD.label: Participant Auditors
AllAuditorsDD.tooltip: The Potential Auditors dropdown will list the members of the selected team(s) for each audit definition. If none of the members are available for the execution of the audit, you may select another qualified user in the Lead Auditor field.

children.caption: Assign the PREDEFINED AUDIT PROGRAM(s) as a part of this Audit
The meeting request was not created. You can send the request again by clicking the button on the right.

Select to mark all requirements as verified

Findings that are selected together in the corresponding dropdown below will create one quality event or CAPA instance.
